summary: Backend all endpoints working (health, device CRUD, code lookup case-insensitive, location ingest, track, geofence CRUD, enter/exit event detection, events list, cascading delete). Frontend Landing/Devices/Tracker/Events all pass. Tracker with geolocation permission granted successfully paired and broadcasted location (last-ping shown). Dashboard and Geofences pages both crash with a Leaflet 'Map container is already initialized' runtime error caused by React.StrictMode double-mounting MapContainer without cleanup.
passed: POST /api/devices creates device with unique 6-char code
POST /api/devices/by-code/{code}/location ingests point and updates last_* fields
Geofence enter/exit event detection fires correctly (exit event verified)
Devices (/devices) create/rename/delete flow works via UI
Tracker (/tracker) pair with valid code shows Paired + Start button; with granted geolocation (48.8583,2.2945), start-btn broadcasts and tracker-last-ping appears
Events (/events) renders empty state (events-empty testid present)

failed.issue: Runtime error 'Map container is already initialized.' — Leaflet MapContainer is re-initialized due to React.StrictMode double-mount in dev. Red error overlay covers the page and [data-testid=dashboard-map] never becomes attached.
failed.evidence: Screenshot shows Uncaught runtime error overlay: 'Map container is already initialized.' at NewClass._initContainer. Selector wait_for_selector('[data-testid=dashboard-map]', state=attached) times out at 15s.

failed.issue: Same 'Map container is already initialized' error. [data-testid=geofence-map] never attaches, so fence create/toggle/delete UI flow cannot be tested.
failed.evidence: Same runtime error overlay reproduced when navigating to /geofences.

action_items: Fix Leaflet re-init crash on Dashboard and Geofences pages. Options: (a) remove React.StrictMode from /app/frontend/src/index.js, or (b) ensure react-leaflet MapContainer is wrapped so its container ref is properly cleaned up on unmount (e.g., key prop or a state guard, or use useRef with map.remove() on cleanup), or (c) memoize MapContainer so the effect doesn't re-init in StrictMode's double-invoke.
After fix, retest /dashboard (map + device-list + telemetry-strip + alerts-panel) and /geofences (map click -> fence-submit -> fence-toggle -> fence-delete).
